Empires & Puzzles Lodius Review: The Berserker of Owl Tower

Lodius burst onto the scene in 2024 as a Very Fast sniper with a terrifying damage cap. Two years later, does his unique stat-based damage mechanic hold up against the massive defense stats of modern heroes? We analyze his viability, his reliance on Goblin synergies, and whether this axe-wielding owl is still a predator or just prey.
Lodius Review: The Very Fast Owl With A Very Big Axe
Lodius: The "Very Fast" Owl Who Needs A Growth Spurt
Lodius arrived in February 2024 as part of the Owl Tower family, bringing a unique damage mechanic that terrified players... for about three months. A

5-star Fire hero, he fits the classic "Sniper" archetype but with a twist: his damage isn't a fixed percentage but a variable number based on stat differences. He is a pure offense-oriented hero designed to delete a single target before they can blink. Two years later, he is a classic example of a "stat-check" hero struggling in a world of stat-creep.
(Funny remark: He is an owl with axes for legs. We assume landing is... complicated.)

Lodius is undeniably cool in a "heavy metal album cover" kind of way. He features a giant snowy owl wearing spiked red armor, with massive battle axes replacing his talons. It’s an aggressive, dynamic pose that screams "I am going to drop on you from the sky." The design is a refreshing departure from the usual knights and wizards, leaning fully into the "Battle Beast" aesthetic. One has to wonder how he perches on a branch without chopping it down instantly.
Here lies the problem. At his release, Lodius's 1042 Attack was formidable. In 2026, where new 5-star heroes (like the Magic Tower or Astral summon heroes) sport base Attacks of
, Lodius is significantly behind the curve.
for a sniper. This is critical because his special skill relies entirely on his Attack stat being higher than the enemy's Defense.
These are essentially 4-star stats by modern standards. He is extremely fragile and will likely die to a single modern sniper shot or a heavy slash attack.
"Battle Claw Strike" is fascinating but flawed in the current meta.

The +40% damage against Holy targets is his saving grace. It gives him a fighting chance to actually hurt modern Yellow tanks.

If he kills the target, nearby enemies get a 3-turn Mana Block. This is an incredibly powerful effect—effectively a game-winner—but it requires him to secure a kill, which is becoming harder and harder to do.
A nice bonus, especially at Very Fast speed, but 60% is relatively low. It won't kill enemies on its own, but it helps chip them down.

To reach his max damage, his Attack needs to significantly exceed the target's Defense. Currently, he starts at a deficit.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Owl family bonus turns negative mana effects into mana generation buffs. This is excellent against heroes like Waterpipe or the myriad of mana-cutting tanks. Having 2+ Owls makes this bonus potent (31% boost), but Lodius is often run solo in red stacks.
Reduce Mana Gain from Special Skills (-80%):
defensive passive. It means if an enemy hits your team with a special that grants mana (like Xnolphod used to, or various mana buffers), they get almost nothing. It applies to
, making him a subtle but powerful counter to mana-engine teams.
. While it helps his survivability slightly, it does
to help his damage scaling. An "Attack Up" or "Special Boost" aether power would have been far superior for his specific mechanic.
Upon release, Lodius was a "must-have" sniper. His 2200 damage cap seemed unreachable but terrifying. He was a staple in Rush Attack wars and a premier flank in standard defenses.

Power creep has hit Lodius harder than almost any other hero type. Because his damage is tied to a stat comparison, every new batch of heroes with higher Defense stats actively nerfs him without SG changing a single line of code. He went from a one-shot machine to a "tickle machine" against modern tanks.
He is no longer a plug-and-play sniper. He is viable
if you can artificially inflate his stats (Growth) or if you are fighting older teams. In top-tier play, he is mostly a liability due to his low stats.
Absolutely essential for his mechanic. You need every single point of Attack to overcome enemy Defense stats and move away from the minimum damage floor.
He remains made of paper. He will die if a stiff breeze hits him.
Keeps him alive long enough to maybe fire twice?
Without Attack nodes, his special skill will do negligible damage to modern heroes, failing to trigger his Mana Block secondary effect. A living Lodius who hits for 400 damage is useless.
You use Lodius to kill things. If he can't kill, he has no purpose. Maximize his Attack at all costs (LB1, LB2, 25 Master Emblems).
6-tile charge is still the gold standard for offense.
If you trigger the kill, stopping mana on two enemies for 3 turns usually wins the match.
The global reduction of mana gain from special skills is an underrated counter to many support heroes.
His damage collapses against high-defense targets (i.e., everyone released in the last year).
extremely low survival stats by 2026 standards.

Great for picking off damaged teams where he can secure a kill easily.
His speed shines here, and the Counterattack counts as a buff.
Useless. His damage cap limits his potential compared to normal percentage-based snipers on Titans.
To make Lodius work in 2026, you
"Growth" modifies the base stats permanently. This is the

way to push Lodius's Attack high enough to consistently hit his 2200 damage cap against modern enemies. Pair him with Pepperflame: fire Pepperflame first to soften and apply Growth, then Lodius finishes the job.
Defense. This widens the gap between Lodius's Attack and the enemy's Defense, drastically increasing his damage output.
Standard Attack buffers like Black Knight or Ares. Their buffs do nothing for his special skill damage.
Heroes with "Defense Growth" (like other Goblins) or extremely high base defense unless you have Withered them first.

If you pulled him early, congrats! He will carry you through Map Stages and low-level Raids effortlessly. His damage cap won't matter against 4-star enemies or Season 1-3 bosses—he will just delete them. Prioritize Attack emblems.

Lodius is a "Project Hero." He requires LB2 and possibly legendary troops to be relevant. Unless you have the Goblin supports (Growth/Wither) to enable him, he is likely better left on the bench in favor of snipers who don't care about the enemy's defense stat as much (like those with fixed % damage or bypass).
"This guy will certainly be nerfed on arrival." —
Spoiler: He wasn't, power creep did the job for them.
"Because the damage mechanic sucks... owls will become outdated a lot faster than regular heroes." —
A forum user predicted exactly what happened. The static numbers couldn't keep up.
Despite his flaws, players loved the "Brave Bird" animation and the sheer satisfaction of the 6-tile delete button when it worked.

Lodius is a Ferrari engine in a rusted chassis. His skill design—Very Fast mana block on kill—is championship material, but his engine (stats) struggles to keep up with the modern race cars. If you have the Goblins to tune him up, he’s a monster. If not, he’s mostly just a cool-looking lawn ornament.
heroes. Otherwise, treat him as a niche sniper for War cleanups.
